commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=05b682dcad463ba489b29a0f5a3ef973a17ed48f)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds text fallbacks for non-text/image MCP content blocks in two Anthropic content-block converters and guards image output on `data` plus `mimeType`.

PR surface: Source +74. Total +74 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: MCP SDK tool results can contain resource_link/resource/audio blocks, OpenClaw currently casts them into text/image-only tool-result content, and Anthropic converters can mishandle leaked non-text/image blocks. I did not run a live Anthropic/Superhuman reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor code changes plus real behavior proof; automation should not take over while the proof gate still depends on the contributor's runtime setup.

target.title: fix(agents): coerce non-text/image MCP tool-result blocks to text (fixes #90710)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90710

When an MCP tool returns a `resource_link`, `resource`, or `audio` content block, both `convertContentBlocks` functions (in `anthropic.ts` and `anthropic-transport-stream.ts`) assumed "not text → image" and produced `{type: "image", source: {media_type: undefined, data: undefined}}`. Anthropic's API rejects this with HTTP 400, and because the malformed block persists in session history, **every subsequent turn in the session also 400s** — the session is permanently poisoned until history reset.

### What changed

- **Added `blockToTextFallback` / `transportBlockToTextFallback` helpers** that convert MCP `resource_link` → `"title: uri"`, embedded `resource` → `resource.text`, and anything else → JSON string.
- **Guarded the image branch** with `block.type === "image" && block.data && block.mimeType` — only valid image content produces an Anthropic image block.
- **Extended the early-return check** to detect `hasNonTextNonImage` blocks, ensuring resource_link/audio blocks trigger the block-array path even without images present.
- Both `convertContentBlocks` functions patched identically.

### Behavior addressed

Anthropic agents no longer get permanently poisoned by a single MCP resource_link tool result. Valid image blocks are preserved; non-image MCP content is safely converted to text.

## Verification

```
node --import tsx -e '...' (source-level blockToTextFallback verification)

=== #90710 Live source verification ===
Node: v24.15.0 | Platform: darwin x64
Timestamp: 2026-06-05T18:06:16.048Z

[1] resource_link fallback: Quarterly Report: file:///report.pdf
    Expected: Quarterly Report: file:///report.pdf
[2] embedded resource fallback: file contents here
    Expected: file contents here
[3] audio block fallback: {"type":"audio","data":"base64...","mimeType":"audio/mp3"}
    Expected: JSON stringified
[4] image block (should stay as image, not use fallback): {"type":"image","data":"iVBORw0KGgo=","mimeType":"image/png"}

RESULT: Fix confirmed.
```

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Anthropic agents no longer get permanently poisoned (400 on every turn) by a single MCP resource_link tool result.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS darwin x64, Node v24.15.0, local source checkout
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  node --import tsx -e '...' (blockToTextFallback source-level verification)
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture shows all 4 fallback paths working correctly. resource_link produces text instead of {media_type: undefined, data: undefined} image block. Valid image blocks are preserved as-is.
- **Observed result after fix**: Before this fix, `convertContentBlocks` coerced MCP resource_link into `{type: "image", source: {media_type: undefined, data: undefined}}` at line 148 (anthropic.ts) and line 287 (anthropic-transport-stream.ts). After the fix, resource_link blocks produce `{type: "text", text: "title: uri"}` via `blockToTextFallback`. Valid image blocks with `data` and `mimeType` are preserved. Session history no longer contains malformed image blocks that trigger Anthropic HTTP 400.
- **What was not tested**: Live Anthropic API call with a real MCP server returning resource_link content (e.g. Superhuman Mail get_attachment).
